title: Halanthium roseum
taxonomy: Halanthium roseum is a genus and species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and surrounding areas. The genus Halanthium is a small group of flowering plants that are often included in the genus Ferula. Halanthium roseum is typically classified as a perennial herb with a distinctive appearance and growth habit. The classification of Halanthium roseum is subject to ongoing research and may be revised as new information becomes available.

intro: Halanthium roseum is a plant species within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as a type of wild carrot or meadow carrot. The health benefits of Halanthium roseum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
